Getting Started

This guide walks you through adding Aegis to your server and getting it running in a few minutes.

1. Invite the Bot

Use the invite link on the home page or join the Discord server to get the official public instance added to your server. Aegis requires the following gateway intents to function: Message Content, Guild Members, and Guild Moderation.

2. Set the Bot's Role Position

Aegis must have a role that sits above the roles of any members it needs to moderate. Drag the Aegis role above your moderator and member roles in Server Settings → Roles. Without this, ban, kick, and timeout commands will fail with a hierarchy error.

3. Configure a Log Channel

Run +config log #channel to point Aegis at a channel where moderation actions will be logged. This is strongly recommended before using any moderation commands.

4. Basic Usage

All commands use the + prefix by default. Here are a few to get started:

+ban <user> [duration] [reason]
+kick <user> [reason]
+mute <user> <duration> [reason]
+warn <user> [reason]

Reply to a user's message before running a command and Aegis will automatically resolve the target, so you don't need to look up their ID.

5. Permissions

Commands are gated by Discord permissions. For example, +ban requires the Ban Members permission. Staff members without the relevant Discord permission will be denied. See the individual command pages in the sidebar for exact permission requirements.

6. Self-Hosting

Prefer to run your own instance? See the Self-Hosting guide for full setup instructions.
